titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina
Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: : «حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ مَا بَيْنَ لاَبَتَيْهَا صَيْدَهَا وَ حَرَّمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا حَوْلَهَا بَرِيداً فِي بَرِيدٍ أَنْ يُخْتَلَى خَلاَهَا أَوْ يُعْضَدَ شَجَرُهَا إِلاَّ عُودَيِ اَلنَّاضِحِ ».
arabic.text: : «حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ مَا بَيْنَ لاَبَتَيْهَا صَيْدَهَا وَ حَرَّمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَا حَوْلَهَا بَرِيداً فِي بَرِيدٍ أَنْ يُخْتَلَى خَلاَهَا أَوْ يُعْضَدَ شَجَرُهَا إِلاَّ عُودَيِ اَلنَّاضِحِ ».

segments:
Hadith.3148 - It is narrated from Zurarah ibn A’yun, from Abu Ja’far (as), who said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) sanctified the area between its two rocky grounds as the sanctuary of Medina, prohibiting its game.
He (sw) also prohibited the area surrounding it—extending a barid by a barid (approximately 22 kilometers by 22 kilometers)—from having its vegetation cleared or its trees cut, except for branches used to drive camels (‘udayn al-nadhih)."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3148 - It is narrated from Zurarah ibn A’yun, from Abu Ja’far (as), who said:
"The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) sanctified the area between its two rocky grounds as the sanctuary of Medina, prohibiting its game.
He (sw) also prohibited the area surrounding it—extending a barid by a barid (approximately 22 kilometers by 22 kilometers)—from having its vegetation cleared or its trees cut, except for branches used to drive camels (‘udayn al-nadhih)."

narratorChain.raw: 3148 - رَوَى زُرَارَةُ بْنُ أَعْيَنَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: 3149 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ لاَبَتَيْهَا مَا أَحَاطَتْ بِهِ اَلْحِرَارُ ».
arabic.text: 3149 - وَ رُوِيَ : «أَنَّ لاَبَتَيْهَا مَا أَحَاطَتْ بِهِ اَلْحِرَارُ ».

segments:
Hadith.3149 - It is narrated:
"Its two rocky grounds (Lābatayhā) encompass what the volcanic lands surround (al-hirar)."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3149 - It is narrated:
"Its two rocky grounds (Lābatayhā) encompass what the volcanic lands surround (al-hirar)."

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: 3150 - وَ رُوِيَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «أَنَّ مَا بَيْنَ لاَبَتَيْهَا مَا بَيْنَ اَلصَّوْرَيْنِ إِلَى اَلثَّنِيَّةِ ».
arabic.text: 3150 - وَ رُوِيَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «أَنَّ مَا بَيْنَ لاَبَتَيْهَا مَا بَيْنَ اَلصَّوْرَيْنِ إِلَى اَلثَّنِيَّةِ ».

segments:
Hadith.3150 - It is narrated in another report:
"The area between the two lava plains (labatayha) is what lies between as-Sawrayn and ath-Thaniyyah."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3150 - It is narrated in another report:
"The area between the two lava plains (labatayha) is what lies between as-Sawrayn and ath-Thaniyyah."

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: : «حَدُّ مَا حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مِنَ اَلْمَدِينَةِ مِنْ رَبَابٍ إِلَى وَاقِمٍ وَ اَلْعُرَيْضِ وَ اَلنَّقْبِ مِنْ قِبَلِ مَكَّةَ ».
arabic.text: : «حَدُّ مَا حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مِنَ اَلْمَدِينَةِ مِنْ رَبَابٍ إِلَى وَاقِمٍ وَ اَلْعُرَيْضِ وَ اَلنَّقْبِ مِنْ قِبَلِ مَكَّةَ ».

segments:
Hadith.3151 - Abu Basir narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The boundaries of what the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) declared sacred from Medina are from Rabab to Waqim, and from al-‘Urays to an-Naqb, in the direction of Mecca."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3151 - Abu Basir narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"The boundaries of what the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) declared sacred from Medina are from Rabab to Waqim, and from al-‘Urays to an-Naqb, in the direction of Mecca."

narratorChain.raw: 3151 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يَحْرُمُ مِنْ صَيْدِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ مَا صِيدَ بَيْنَ اَلْحَرَّتَيْنِ ».
arabic.text: رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يَحْرُمُ مِنْ صَيْدِ اَلْمَدِينَةِ مَا صِيدَ بَيْنَ اَلْحَرَّتَيْنِ ».

segments:
Hadith.3152 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"It is prohibited to hunt in Medina what is hunted between the two Harras (rocky tracts)."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3152 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrates from Abu Abdullah (as):
"It is prohibited to hunt in Medina what is hunted between the two Harras (rocky tracts)."

narratorChain.raw: وَفِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ:

parts.text: َفِي رِوَايَةِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ:

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: : يَحْرُمُ عَلَيَّ فِي حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مَا يَحْرُمُ عَلَيَّ فِي حَرَمِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى قَالَ «لاَ».
arabic.text: : يَحْرُمُ عَلَيَّ فِي حَرَمِ رَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ مَا يَحْرُمُ عَلَيَّ فِي حَرَمِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى قَالَ «لاَ».

segments:
Hadith.3153 - Yunus ibn Ya’qub asked Imam (as):
"Is what is prohibited for me in the sanctuary of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) the same as what is prohibited for me in the sanctuary of Allah (swt), the Exalted?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3153 - Yunus ibn Ya’qub asked Imam (as):
"Is what is prohibited for me in the sanctuary of the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) the same as what is prohibited for me in the sanctuary of Allah (swt), the Exalted?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."

narratorChain.raw: 3153 - وَ سَأَلَهُ يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ قَالَ

parts.text: 3153 - وَ سَأَلَهُ يُونُسُ بْنُ يَعْقُوبَ قَالَ

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ حَرَّمَ بَرِيداً فِي بَرِيدٍ عِضَاهاً» قُلْتُ صَيْدَهَا قَالَ «لاَ يَكْذِبُ اَلنَّاسُ.
arabic.text: : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ حَرَّمَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ حَرَّمَ بَرِيداً فِي بَرِيدٍ عِضَاهاً» قُلْتُ صَيْدَهَا قَالَ «لاَ يَكْذِبُ اَلنَّاسُ.

segments:
Hadith.3154 - Aban narrated from Abu al-‘Abbas, meaning al-Fadl ibn Abd al-Malik, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), "Did the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) declare Medina a sanctuary?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, he declared an area of a barid (about 22 kilometers) by a barid as a sanctuary, forbidding the cutting of its of its ʿIdhāhāʾ (trees)."
I then asked: "What about its hunting?"
Imam (as) replied: "People do not lie."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3154 - Aban narrated from Abu al-‘Abbas, meaning al-Fadl ibn Abd al-Malik, who said:
I asked Abu Abdullah (as), "Did the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) declare Medina a sanctuary?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, he declared an area of a barid (about 22 kilometers) by a barid as a sanctuary, forbidding the cutting of its of its ʿIdhāhāʾ (trees)."
I then asked: "What about its hunting?"
Imam (as) replied: "People do not lie."

narratorChain.raw: 3154 - وَ رَوَى أَبَانٌ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْعَبَّاسِ يَعْنِي اَلْفَضْلَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ قَالَ

parts.text: أَبِي اَلْعَبَّاسِ يَعْنِي اَلْفَضْلَ بْنَ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: 3155 - وَ لَمَّا دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ قَالَ « اَللَّهُمَّ حَبِّبْ إِلَيْنَا اَلْمَدِينَةَ كَمَا حَبَّبْتَ إِلَيْنَا مَكَّةَ أَوْ أَشَدَّ وَ بَارِكْ فِي صَاعِهَا وَ مُدِّهَا وَ اُنْقُلْ حُمَّاهَا وَ وَبَاهَا إِلَى اَلْجُحْفَةِ ».
arabic.text: 3155 - وَ لَمَّا دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ قَالَ « اَللَّهُمَّ حَبِّبْ إِلَيْنَا اَلْمَدِينَةَ كَمَا حَبَّبْتَ إِلَيْنَا مَكَّةَ أَوْ أَشَدَّ وَ بَارِكْ فِي صَاعِهَا وَ مُدِّهَا وَ اُنْقُلْ حُمَّاهَا وَ وَبَاهَا إِلَى اَلْجُحْفَةِ ».

segments:
Hadith.3155 - When the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) entered Medina, he said:
"O Allah (swt), make Medina beloved to us as You made Mecca beloved to us, or even more so. Bless its sa‘ (a measure of weight) and its mudd (a smaller measure of weight), and transfer its fever and pestilence to al-Juhfa."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3155 - When the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) entered Medina, he said:
"O Allah (swt), make Medina beloved to us as You made Mecca beloved to us, or even more so. Bless its sa‘ (a measure of weight) and its mudd (a smaller measure of weight), and transfer its fever and pestilence to al-Juhfa."

narratorChain.raw: 3155 - وَ لَمَّا دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ اَلْمَدِينَةَ قَالَ

parts.text: لَمَّا دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ

titles.en: Chapter on the Sanctity and Virtue of Medina

segments: 3156 - وَ رُوِيَ : أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ذَكَرَ اَلدَّجَّالَ فَقَالَ «لاَ يَبْقَى مِنْهَا سَهْلٌ إِلاَّ وَطِئَهُ إِلاَّ مَكَّةَ وَ اَلْمَدِينَةَ فَإِنَّ عَلَى كُلِّ نَقْبٍ مِنْ أَنْقَابِهِمَا مَلَكاً يَحْفَظُهُمَا مِنَ اَلطَّاعُونِ وَ اَلدَّجَّالِ ».
arabic.text: 3156 - وَ رُوِيَ : أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ ذَكَرَ اَلدَّجَّالَ فَقَالَ «لاَ يَبْقَى مِنْهَا سَهْلٌ إِلاَّ وَطِئَهُ إِلاَّ مَكَّةَ وَ اَلْمَدِينَةَ فَإِنَّ عَلَى كُلِّ نَقْبٍ مِنْ أَنْقَابِهِمَا مَلَكاً يَحْفَظُهُمَا مِنَ اَلطَّاعُونِ وَ اَلدَّجَّالِ ».

segments:
Hadith.3156 - It is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) mentioned the Dajjal and said:
"There will not remain any plain area except that he will tread upon it, except Mecca and Medina, for at every entrance to them, there is an angel protecting them from plague and the Dajjal."
en.bab-ul-qaim-publications.text:
Hadith.3156 - It is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) mentioned the Dajjal and said:
"There will not remain any plain area except that he will tread upon it, except Mecca and Medina, for at every entrance to them, there is an angel protecting them from plague and the Dajjal."
